title: あなたはCode Reviewerです。以下の観点でレビューします。 ## 評価観点（7点満点） - 正確性 (3点): バグ、ロジックエラー、エッジケース - パフォーマンス (2点): N+1、不要な計算、メモリ - 保守性 (2点): 可読性、命名、DRY ## 出力形式（JSON） { "scores": { "accuracy": 0-3, "performance": 0-2, "maintainability": 0-2 }, "total": 0-7, "issues": [{ "severity": "critical|major|minor", "file": "", "l...
summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 1, "performance": 1, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 3, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "lib/problem-solver.js", "line": "287-344", "description": "Verification phase rewrites safety-sensitive keywords such as `production`, `main`, `credentials`, `delete all`, and `rm -rf` before sending the execution result to the vote engine. That avoids false positives, but it also suppresses the exact signals that should force human confirmation on genuinely dangerous outputs. A risky fix can therefore be auto-verified after its most important warning terms have been removed.", "suggestion": "Do not sanitize away Level-1 keywords before verification. Instead, pass raw output to the vote engine and fix the false-positive problem at the matcher level, for example by switching from substring matching to word-boundary or token-based matching." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "task-scheduler.js", "line": "63-64", "description": "`today` is computed in UTC via `toISOString()`, while `dayOfWeek` is computed in local time via `getDay()`. Around local midnight this can create or skip weekly tasks on the wrong date, and `.last-scheduler-run.json` will persist the wrong execution day.", "suggestion": "Use a single timezone source for both values. If this scheduler is intended to run in the machine's local timezone, derive both the date key and weekday from the same local `Date` instance or explicitly use a configured timezone." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "lib/github-client.js", "line": "54-62", "description": "Issue polling is hard-limited to 10 open issues. When more than 10 labeled issues are open, older unprocessed items are never fetched, so the pipeline can silently starve tasks instead of draining the queue.", "suggestion": "Paginate until exhaustion or use a substantially higher configurable limit. At minimum, sort deterministically and keep fetching until all matching open issues have been checked against state." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "lib/task-reporter.js", "line": "169-173", "description": "The problem-solver final report uses overall pipeline success to render the `Execution` row. If execution succeeded but verification later rejected the result, the report will incorrectly say `Execution | Failed`, which misstates what actually happened and makes debugging harder.", "suggestion": "Report phase status from phase-specific state, for example from `pipeline.history` or a dedicated `executionPhaseSuccess` field, rather than reusing the final pipeline success flag." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "lib/task-executor.js", "line": "95-116", "description": "Execution reloads `config.json` on every task and ignores problem-solver execution-specific controls such as `problemSolver.phases.execution.timeout` and `maxTokensPerTask`. This makes behavior harder to reason about and can allow unnecessarily large prompts or longer-than-expected child runs.", "suggestion": "Pass validated config into `executeTask()` from the caller, enforce a single timeout source, and apply prompt-size guards before spawning child processes." }, { "severity": "minor", "file": "task-scheduler.js", "line": "41-56", "description": "`runGh()` captures only stdout and drops stderr, so operational failures lose their most useful diagnostics. In practice this makes auth, rate-limit, or validation failures much harder to triage.", "suggestion": "Capture stderr and include it in the rejection message, matching the more robust wrapper already used in `lib/github-client.js`." } ], "summary": "Assumption: this review covers first-party JS under `~/.claude/auto-task` and excludes vendored `node_modules`. The highest-risk problem is in the verification flow: safety keywords are intentionally removed before consensus voting, which weakens the human-confirmation guardrail for dangerous outputs. The main correctness risks after that are queue starvation from the 10-issue fetch cap, date drift in the scheduler due to mixed UTC/l...
